dataUpdates.proposed_value: (1) Standards development bottleneck: Only EN 197-1 has a registered prEN (stage 10.99, Jul 2025). The remaining 8 product standards (EN 413-1, EN 459-1, EN 15368, EN 14216, EN 14647, EN 15743, EN 13282, EN 16908) have SReq delivery deadlines in 2027–2028 but no prEN registrations yet. CEN first annual report (due Jun 2026) will clarify progress. (2) Alkali-activated cements: CEN/TC 51 refused to develop standards; downgraded to Technical Report in C(2025) 4828. Performance-based standard requested for delivery by 2030 but uncertain. (3) EAD sunset risk: EAD 150080-00-0301 (blended cements) valid only until 9 Jan 2031 under old CPR. New-regime EAD needed for continued ETA route. EOTA mandate by 2027 signaled but not yet formalised. (4) Clean Industrial Deal: Mandatory cement carbon label (GWP disclosure in DoP) planned when new standards become mandatory, adding compliance complexity.
dataUpdates.reason: key_risks field was empty. Deep-dive research identified four material risks for CEM family: standards development bottleneck, alkali-activated cement refusal, EAD sunset, and CID carbon label.
dataUpdates.source: Deep-dive analysis of SReq C(2025) 4828, genorma.com (prEN 197-1 stage), joint letter May 2025, Ecocem press Dec 2025, CEMBUREAU CID references

structuralIssues.title: FEICA Model EPDs provide DPP-ready environmental data infrastructure for SME-dominated adhesives industry
structuralIssues.description: FEICA Model EPDs (since 2016, updated 2022/2024 jointly with IVK, Deutsche Bauchemie, EFCC) are structured by chemical composition, not product-specific. Third-party verified per EN 15804+A2 by IBU. Any FEICA/IVK/DBC/EFCC member can declare compliance. This covers ~800 companies (90% SME). Sika EPDs since 2012 (BRE-verified), Mapei EPD Process Certified since 2016. ADH is among most DPP-ready families via FEICA collective approach — directly applicable to DPP environmental data.
structuralIssues.source: FEICA EPDs (https://www.feica.eu/our-projects/epds); FEICA Model EPDs (https://www.feica.eu/information-center/latest-news/Model-EPDs); EFCC 2024 update (https://www.efcc.eu/our-actions/guidance-tools-industry/updated-2024-european-model-environmental-product-declarations)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document FEICA Model EPD programme in content. Flag as DPP readiness exemplar for SME-dominated families.

structuralIssues.title: REACH diisocyanate restriction reshapes PU adhesive market — DPP chemical content implications
structuralIssues.description: REACH restriction on diisocyanates (24 Aug 2023): products >0.1% diisocyanates require mandatory user training. Affects polyurethane construction adhesives. Industry shifting to non-PU structural adhesives (epoxy, acrylic) and reformulating to <0.1%. DPP cross-regulatory: adhesive DPP must declare diisocyanate content, training requirements, VOC emissions alongside mechanical performance. CEN/TC 351 dangerous substances will mandate chemical content declarations.
structuralIssues.source: REACH restriction (https://www.forgeway.com/learning/blog/isocyanate-restrictions-2023); KLEIBERIT (https://www.kleiberit.com/en/company/news/isocyanate-restrictions-in-the-european-union); Adhesives Expo (https://www.adhesivesandbondingexpo-europe.com/blogs/growing-demand-for-non-pu-structural-adhesives-after-eu-diisocyanates-rule)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document diisocyanate restriction in content.key_risks. Flag CEN/TC 351 as DPP data requirement.

structuralIssues.title: ADH scope (EN 12004 only) covers fraction of €19.9B adhesives market — SReq may expand significantly
structuralIssues.description: Current ADH tracks only EN 12004 tile adhesives (2 hENs). European adhesives/sealants market: €19.9B, 4.8Mt, 800 companies (FEICA data). Global tile adhesive $3.6B, flooring adhesive $5.6B. Future SReq (2029) could expand to floor covering adhesives, structural bonding, gypsum adhesives (EN 12860). Deutsche Bauchemie (140 companies, €4.6B sales) actively positioned on CPR/sustainability including Circular Economy Act and Housing Strategy.
structuralIssues.source: FEICA (https://www.feica.eu/our-industry); Deutsche Bauchemie (https://deutsche-bauchemie.com/); Grand View Research (https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/tile-adhesive-market-report)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and potential SReq scope expansion in content.

structuralIssues.title: prEN 17555-1 consolidation status unclear
structuralIssues.description: CEN/TC 154 is working on prEN 17555-1 to consolidate multiple aggregate standards (EN 12620, EN 13043, EN 13139, EN 13242, possibly EN 13055) into a single comprehensive standard. The project reached CEN Enquiry stage (~40.60) around 2021, but current status is unclear. Additionally, EN 12620 has had a formal objection pending since ~2015. If the consolidation succeeds, the standards landscape would change dramatically from 7 hENs to potentially 1–2.
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor prEN 17555-1 progress. If consolidation is adopted, update standards[] and counts. Check formal objection resolution for EN 12620.

structuralIssues.title: MPA sector EPD model as DPP template — individual quarry EPDs also emerging across Europe
structuralIssues.description: UK MPA's sector EPD programme covers 90% of aggregates production with collective industry declarations per EN 15804+A2. Individual quarry EPDs appearing: Interbeton limestone (Greece), Trzuskawica basalt (Poland, ITB-verified), OCO carbon-negative aggregates (UK, from waste CO₂). Industry's 15,000 companies (mostly SME quarries) likely need collective/sector EPD approach rather than individual declarations for DPP compliance. MPA model could serve as EU-wide DPP data template.
structuralIssues.source: MPA EPDs (https://www.concretecentre.com/Codes/Environmental-Assessment/EPD.aspx); MPA asphalt EPDs (https://mineralproducts.org/News/2024/release17.aspx); Interbeton EPD (https://interbeton.gr/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/EPD-_Latomeio-Xirorematos.pdf); OCO Technology EPD (https://oco.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Environmental-Product-Declaration.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document sector EPD model in content. Flag as DPP readiness exemplar for SME-dominated families.

structuralIssues.title: CDW recycled aggregates — 1/3 of EU waste, end-of-waste criteria needed for DPP recycled content
structuralIssues.description: Construction and demolition waste (CDW) is 1/3 of all EU waste — largest waste stream. WFD 70% recovery target met largely through backfilling/low-grade use, not high-quality recycling. EuRIC advocates EU-level end-of-waste regulations. EAD 240002 (MSWI bottom ash aggregates) and OCO carbon-negative aggregates demonstrate circular pathways. DPP for recycled aggregates needs: waste origin, processing method, end-of-waste certification, performance data. Carbon-negative products may show negative GWP — DPP data fields must handle this.
structuralIssues.source: EEA CDW (https://www.eea.europa.eu/highlights/improving-circular-economy-practices-in); EC CDW policy (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/construction-and-demolition-waste_en); EC end-of-waste (https://circular-cities-and-regions.ec.europa.eu/support-materials/projects/end-waste-criteria-protocol-recycled-waste-used-aggregates)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document CDW circular economy dimension in content. Note end-of-waste criteria as DPP data architecture requirement.

structuralIssues.title: 3 billion tonnes/year, $61-106B market, 15,000 companies — largest-by-mass CPR family, most fragmented
structuralIssues.description: European aggregates: 3 billion tonnes annual production, $61.4-105.7B market (2024), 15,000 companies at 26,000 sites, 187,000 employees. Largest CPR product family by physical mass (orders of magnitude above cement ~185 Mt, asphalt ~209 Mt, or steel). Most fragmented producer base — overwhelmingly SME quarries. UEPG 2030 Roadmap addresses CPR and sustainability. DPP implementation uniquely challenging for this industry structure.
structuralIssues.source: UEPG/Aggregates Europe (https://www.aggregates-europe.eu/); Fortune Business Insights (https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/europe-construction-aggregates-market-104698); Credence Research (https://www.credenceresearch.com/report/europe-construction-aggregates-market)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market scale and SME challenge in content for DPP impact assessment.

structuralIssues.title: Prysmian and Nexans publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs — cable copper 100% recyclable, PCR NPCR 027 exists
structuralIssues.description: Prysmian publishes EN 15804+A2 EPDs for cable products (PCR NPCR 027.2022). Nexans produces EPDs per EN 15804+A2 (PCR ed4). Copper is 100% recyclable with no quality loss. PVC recycled with 90% primary energy savings vs virgin. XLPE chemical recycling emerging (Borealis Borcycle™ C). VinylPlus/PVC4Cables and RecyCâbles coordinate cable recovery across Europe. For DPP: market leaders have EPD infrastructure but hundreds of SME cable manufacturers likely lack EPD coverage. Cable-specific PCR exists, which is positive for DPP data standardisation.
structuralIssues.source: Prysmian EPD (https://northeurope.prysmian.com/sustainability/products-services/epd); Nexans EPD (https://www.nexans.no/en/sustainability/Miljo/PEP.html); PVC4Cables (https://pvc4cables.org/sustainability/circular-economy/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness and cable recycling infrastructure in content.

structuralIssues.title: REACH lead restriction on PVC cables (Nov 2024) and PFAS proposal — EN 50575 scope expansion likely most significant for any single-hEN family
structuralIssues.description: REACH lead restriction on PVC articles effective Nov 29, 2024 directly affects PVC-insulated cables. ECHA PFAS restriction proposal could impact fluoropolymer cable insulation (PTFE, FEP, ETFE). PVC cables release up to 30% HCl when burned (driving LSZH adoption). Ireland mandates LSZH; Netherlands, Germany, Greece, Switzerland require LSZH in high-risk environments. Current EN 50575 covers reaction to fire only — dangerous substances dimension conspicuously absent. SReq scope expansion to include dangerous substances, durability, and recyclability would represent one of the most significant expansions for any single-hEN family.
structuralIssues.source: SGS EU lead in PVC (https://www.sgs.com/en-us/news/2023/05/safeguards-5523-eu-restricts-lead-in-pvc-articles-under-reach); UL PFAS (https://www.ul.com/news/eu-sets-pfas-restrictions-consumer-products); Europacable (https://cpr.europacable.eu/en/faq)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document dangerous substances regulatory convergence in content.key_risks. Flag as critical SReq scope expansion risk.

structuralIssues.title: European wire/cable market $36–63B (2024) — construction cables a subset, growth driven by electrification and renewables
structuralIssues.description: European wire and cable market $36.3–63.3B (2024, scope varies), CAGR 5.2–7.2%. Construction building cables (EN 50575 scope) are a subset of the broader market. Growth drivers: renewable energy, EV charging infrastructure, smart grid modernisation, EPBD building electrification. Key manufacturers: Prysmian (world's largest), Nexans, NKT, Leoni. Cable recycling well-established — copper 100% recyclable, PVC recovery programmes, XLPE chemical recycling emerging. CENELEC (not CEN) is the standardisation body — unique among CPR families.
structuralIssues.source: GMInsights (https://www.gminsights.com/industry-analysis/europe-wire-and-cable-market); Mordor Intelligence (https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-wire-and-cable-market)

structuralIssues.title: Industrial Decarbonisation Accelerator Act (IDAA) — postponed, will operationalise cement carbon label
structuralIssues.description: The IDAA was initially scheduled 10 Dec 2025, postponed twice, expected 25 Feb 2026. It will operationalise the cement carbon label mandating GWP disclosure (kgCO2 eq./t) in Declaration of Performance and Conformity via CPR. EPBD requires lifecycle GWP for buildings >1000m² from 2028, all new buildings from 2030. Germany already has national cement carbon labelling. CEMBUREAU (now Cement Europe) supports lead markets approach.
structuralIssues.source: CemNet Dec 2025, EU Parliament legislative train, Cement Europe position paper Jun 2025
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor IDAA adoption. When adopted, assess impact on CEM DPP data requirements and timeline. Update content sections.

structuralIssues.title: EN 197-5 (CEM VI) NOT harmonised — low-carbon cements excluded from DPP
structuralIssues.description: EN 197-5:2021 (CEM II/C-M and CEM VI ternary cements, ~340 kg CO₂/t) is NOT harmonised under CPR and has no OJ citation. CEM VI cements must use national approvals (e.g., Dyckerhoff received German DIBt abZ 20 Oct 2025). This excludes the most climate-relevant cements from the harmonised CE marking and DPP system. The prEN 197-1 revision (stage 10.99 since Jul 2025) is the only path to harmonisation but is years away. ALCCC advocates for performance-based standards to close this gap.
structuralIssues.source: EN 197-5:2021 (https://standards.iteh.ai/catalog/standards/cen/69d3b559-4114-43b3-bfed-05787fc839a2/en-197-5-2021); Dyckerhoff CEM VI abZ (https://www.dyckerhoff.com/en/w/zulassung-cem-vi-zement); ALCCC (https://alliancelccc.com/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Flag in content.key_risks — low-carbon cements excluded from harmonised system. Monitor prEN 197-1 revision progress for CEM VI inclusion.

structuralIssues.title: EPBD delegated act C(2025) 8723 creates building-level GWP demand for cement data
structuralIssues.description: Commission delegated regulation C(2025) 8723 (16 Dec 2025) establishes EU framework for building life-cycle GWP calculations: >1000m² from Jan 2028, all new buildings from Jan 2030. Data hierarchy favours product-specific EPDs over generic values. Cement is a major contributor to building embodied carbon. Creates downstream pull for plant-specific digital EPD data — exactly what DPP requires. Member States must publish GWP limit value roadmaps by 2027.
structuralIssues.source: C(2025) 8723 (https://energy.ec.europa.eu/document/download/a13f9997-fe46-40ec-b158-86149e978624_en?filename=C_2025_8723_1_EN_ACT_part1_v5.pdf); EC announcement (https://energy.ec.europa.eu/news/commission-encourages-low-carbon-construction-materials-calculation-framework-life-cycle-global-2025-12-16_en)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document in content.key_risks. Cross-reference with iss-DWS-009 and other families affected by EPBD GWP requirements.

structuralIssues.title: EU ETS binder-based benchmark shift + CBAM makes GWP disclosure economically consequential
structuralIssues.description: From 1 Jan 2026, ETS free allocations shift from clinker-based to binder-based benchmark (Cement Europe flags as 'unclear'). Free allocation phase-out: 97.5% remaining 2026, 51.5% by 2030, 0% by 2034. ETS benchmark: 0.666 t CO₂/t clinker. CBAM definitive compliance started 1 Jan 2026. The progressive cost increase for carbon makes CPR GWP disclosure on DPPs economically consequential — low-carbon cements gain real cost advantage. Two carbon accounting methodologies (ETS/CBAM and CPR/EN 15804) must align.
structuralIssues.source: Cement Europe EU ETS page (https://www.cementeurope.eu/policy-focus/climate-co2-strategy/eu-ets/); CBAM timeline (https://www.coolset.com/academy/cbam-timeline-deadlines-phases-what-to-expect-2026); SCHWENK 2026 seminars (https://www.schwenk.de/en/schwenk-concrete-seminars-2026-transformation-with-realism/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document ETS/CBAM interaction in content. Note methodological alignment risk between ETS binder-based and CPR EN 15804-based carbon accounting.

structuralIssues.title: EN 12391-1 superseded by EN 15287-1:2007 — likely not harmonised

Execution standards are not harmonised under the CPR. Combined with EN 1443 being a general requirements/umbrella standard, the hen_count correction from 18 to 16 is increasingly likely. Strengthens iss-CHI-001.

structuralIssues.title: Only Schiedel has chimney EPD — industry EPD readiness very low
structuralIssues.description: Schiedel GmbH has an EPD for the Absolut Parat chimney system (EN 15804, 2021 data), but no other chimney manufacturer EPDs were found. DPP GWP declaration under new CPR will require EN 15804-based environmental data. Very low EPD readiness across the chimney industry compared to cement, concrete, or steel sectors.
structuralIssues.source: Schiedel EPD (https://www.heinze.de/m2/17/61317/pdf/53/47852553px595x842.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness gap in content. Flag as risk for CPR GWP disclosure compliance.

structuralIssues.title: Heat pump sales overtook gas boilers in Germany 2025 — structural chimney demand shift
structuralIssues.description: In Germany 2025, 299,000 heat pumps sold (+55%) vs 229,000 gas boilers (−36%). EU air-water heat pump sales grew 275% from 2015 to 2024. EPBD pushing fossil boiler phase-out. Structural demand shift from gas/oil flues toward biomass and condensing flues. Affects DPP system sizing assumptions and product mix within chimney family.
structuralIssues.source: Heatpumpswatch.org (https://heatpumpswatch.org/heating-technologies-compared/); IEA Future of Heat Pumps (https://www.iea.org/reports/the-future-of-heat-pumps/executive-summary); Clean Energy Wire (https://www.cleanenergywire.org/news/rising-heating-costs-2025-impact-homes-gas-boilers-most-analysis)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document market shift in content. Consider impact on DPP product scope and volume estimates.

structuralIssues.title: No hydrogen-ready chimney/flue standard despite EU hydrogen strategy
structuralIssues.description: No specific hydrogen-ready chimney or flue standard found. Hydrogen combustion produces different flue gas: higher water vapour, different condensate chemistry. Current EN 1443 designation system (T/P/W-D/V/G-O/L) designed for conventional fuels. As EU hydrogen deployment accelerates (network blending, dedicated H2 appliances), chimney standards may need hydrogen-specific designation classes. Gap could delay DPP-ready harmonised standards if revision needed.
structuralIssues.source: BFCMA (https://www.bfcma.co.uk/regulations-and-standards); ChimneyLab.dk (https://www.chimneylab.dk/engelsk/chimneys/standards-for-chimney-testing)
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor CEN/TC 166 for hydrogen-related work items. Document gap in content.key_risks.

structuralIssues.title: Bridge infrastructure EADs overlap with SBE domain
structuralIssues.description: Multiple CIF EADs (120093, 120109, 120112) cover bridge infrastructure (expansion joints, parapets) that overlaps with SBE's structural bearings domain. Not a data error — the products are correctly classified under PA 12 (road equipment) — but the domain overlap should be documented for cross-family consistency monitoring.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document CIF/SBE bridge infrastructure overlap. No data changes needed.

structuralIssues.title: EPD adoption early-stage for road equipment — ArcelorMittal and Unipromet are pioneers
structuralIssues.description: ArcelorMittal obtained EPD for single-sided road barriers (HSLA steel, Magnelis coating). Unipromet published EPD for noise protection panels per EN 15804+A2 (2025). EPD adoption in road equipment is early-stage compared to building materials — driven by green public procurement in Scandinavia/Benelux. System 1 AVCP dominance (80% of CIF standards) means structured crash test data already exists and is well-suited for DPP integration. New CPR 2024/3110 environmental sustainability indicators will require road equipment manufacturers to develop EPD/LCA data — a significant shift for this safety-focused sector.
structuralIssues.source: ArcelorMittal EPD (https://europe.arcelormittal.com/newsandmedia/europenews/3170/EPDroadbarriers); Unipromet EPD (https://unipromet.co.rs/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/EPD_HUB-4012_2025-Nois-protection-panels.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document emerging EPD landscape in content. Note System 1 data infrastructure as DPP advantage.

structuralIssues.title: EN 12966 VMS face CPR + EMC + LVD + potentially Cyber Resilience Act — compound regulatory burden
structuralIssues.description: Variable message signs (EN 12966) require CPR CE marking plus EMC Directive (2014/30/EU) and Low Voltage Directive (2014/35/EU) compliance. As connected ITS infrastructure, VMS may also fall under Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) from 2025 — requiring cybersecurity assessment. This makes VMS potentially the most multi-directive product in CIF, similar to FFF fire alarm systems (Batch 2). DPP data declarations for VMS would need to span construction product performance, electrical safety, EMC, and potentially cybersecurity — a compound data architecture challenge.
structuralIssues.source: EN 12966 (https://standards.iteh.ai/catalog/standards/cen/b80b24ba-0bb1-4964-8c3e-d69cde1d4510/en-12966-2014); ARTSM VMS guidance (https://artsm.org.uk/media/Guidance-on-the-selection-of-VMS-updated-v3.1-FINAL.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document VMS multi-directive complexity in content.key_risks. Cross-reference with FFF multi-directive issues.

structuralIssues.title: European road safety barriers $1.8B (2024) — safety-critical family with System 1 dominance
structuralIssues.description: European highway safety barrier market ~$1.8B (2024). Europe holds 26-29% of global road safety system market. Growth drivers: EU 50% fatality reduction target by 2030, TEN-T upgrades, smart motorway deployments. Steel guardrails dominate by volume. CIF is mid-sized CPR family but with high compliance costs per product due to System 1 crash testing requirements (~€100k+ per full-scale test). Steel barriers benefit from 100% recyclability narrative for DPP circular economy data.
structuralIssues.source: Road safety barrier market (https://www.wiseguyreports.com/reports/road-safety-barrier-market); Grand View Research (https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/barrier-systems-market)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and System 1 compliance costs in content.

structuralIssues.title: EN 998-1 and EN 998-2 cross-listed with MAS — data inconsistencies
structuralIssues.description: EN 998-1 appears in both CMG and MAS with inconsistent data. AVCP: CMG records '2+, 4' while MAS records '4'. Name: CMG says 'Specification for mortar for masonry' while MAS says 'Mortar for masonry'. DPP estimates also differ (~2032–2033 vs ~2031–2032). EN 998-2 has matching AVCP but different DPP estimates. The correct AVCP for EN 998-1 is '2+, 4' per Commission Decision 97/555/EC (System 2+ for designed mortars, System 4 for prescribed). MAS data likely has an error.
structuralIssues.action_required: Reconcile EN 998-1 and EN 998-2 data across CMG and MAS. Verify AVCP against Commission Decision 97/555/EC. Align name format. DPP estimates may legitimately differ per family timeline.

structuralIssues.title: EN 206-1:2026 ratified — potential CPR scope expansion to ready-mixed concrete
structuralIssues.description: EN 206-1:2026 was ratified by CEN/TC 104 (DOR 19 January 2026). EN 206 is currently a specification standard, NOT harmonised under CPR. If the future CMG SReq (targeted Q4 2026) brings EN 206 into CPR scope, it would be transformational: the ready-mixed concrete market (~€80B+ annually in Europe) would require CE marking and ultimately DPP compliance. This is the single largest potential scope expansion in the entire CPR DPP programme.
structuralIssues.source: Genorma EN 206-1:2026 (https://genorma.com/en/standards/en-206-1-2026)
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor CMG SReq scope decisions closely. If EN 206 is included, DPP range and market impact estimates need major revision.

structuralIssues.title: Split EPD readiness: admixtures excellent (EFCA since 2005), EN 1504 repair products lack EPDs
structuralIssues.description: EFCA has published admixture EPDs since 2005 (EN 15804 + ISO 14025), covering plasticisers, superplasticisers, accelerators, retarders, air entrainers, waterproofers. Sika EPDs since 2012, Master Builders Solutions EPDs including 50% lower-carbon grout. However, EN 1504 concrete repair/protection products (19 EADs in CMG) appear to lack EPDs. Split readiness within the family: admixtures are DPP-ready, repair products are not.
structuralIssues.source: EFCA EPDs (https://www.efca.info/admixtures/environmental-and-sustainability-aspects/environmental-product-declarations-epd/); Sika LCA (https://gbr.sika.com/en/sustainability/sustainable-solutions/lca-and-epd-s.html)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document split readiness in content. Note EFCA as DPP-ready exemplar for construction chemicals.

structuralIssues.title: Concrete Europe (BIBM+CEMBUREAU+EFCA+ERMCO) coordinates across CEM, CMG, PCR, MAS
structuralIssues.description: Four European concrete sector associations formed 'Concrete Europe' as coordinated umbrella: BIBM (precast/PCR), CEMBUREAU (cement/CEM), EFCA (admixtures/CMG), ERMCO (ready-mixed/CMG). The concrete value chain spans 4 CPR families. DPP data architecture should align across the chain: cement GWP → admixture GWP → concrete mix GWP → precast product GWP. Sika already has LCA tools linking component EPD data into concrete mix design GWP.
structuralIssues.source: Concrete Europe (https://www.theconcreteinitiative.eu/about-us); ERMCO (https://ermco.eu/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Ensure DPP data model consistency across CEM, CMG, PCR, MAS families. Reference Concrete Europe coordination.

structuralIssues.title: Schüco, Reynaers, ALUMIL, WICONA all publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs — among strongest EPD coverage in CPR
structuralIssues.description: Schüco publishes EN 15804+A2 EPDs for aluminium profiles and FWS 60 curtain wall systems (German Sustainability Award 2026 winner). Reynaers publishes EPDs for ConceptWall® 50. ALUMIL publishes EPDs for M7 curtain wall. WICONA publishes EPDs for WICTEC 50. All use 1 m² declared unit, modules A1-C4 (cradle-to-grave). Aluminium sector invested €700M in recycling facilities. 90%+ recycled content facades already built. For DPP: aluminium curtain wall sector has among the strongest EPD coverage of any CPR family — full lifecycle data already available from major manufacturers.
structuralIssues.source: Schüco EPD Guide (https://epd.guide/manufacturers/schuco-product-range-and-epd-coverage-in-2025); Reynaers EPD (https://european-aluminium.eu/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/EPD-Reynaers_Concept-Wall-50.pdf); European Aluminium (https://european-aluminium.eu/policy_areas/circular-economy/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness in content. Flag as DPP-ready exemplar — one of the most prepared families.

structuralIssues.title: Post-Grenfell facade fire testing may expand CPR essential characteristics — EN 1364-4 gaps identified
structuralIssues.description: Post-Grenfell regulatory response driving large-scale facade fire testing requirements (BS 8414 in UK, similar proposals in EU). Academic review of EN 1364-4 (fire resistance of partial curtain wall configurations) identified gaps: fire propagation within facade system 'not necessarily considered in fire-resistant spandrel zone.' EN 13830 defines fire propagation resistance as essential characteristic (E, EI, EW) but whole-system behaviour testing remains incomplete. Several EU Member States developing similar large-scale test requirements. For DPP: fire performance data scope may expand beyond current EN 13830 declarations to include whole-system fire propagation testing.
structuralIssues.source: MDPI fire safety research (https://www.mdpi.com/2571-6255/8/8/311); BS 8414 (https://www.designingbuildings.co.uk/wiki/BS_8414_Fire_performance_of_external_cladding_systems); Intertek EN 13830 (https://www.intertek.com/building/standards/en-13830/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document fire safety regulatory evolution in content.key_risks. Flag EN 1364-4 gaps as potential driver for expanded DPP fire data.

structuralIssues.title: Global facades $298B (2025), curtain walls 45% of European value — aluminium circular economy strong
structuralIssues.description: Global facade market $298.20B (2025), projected $389.70B by 2030. Curtain walls captured 45.2% of European facade market value (2024). Germany leads 22.1%. Aluminium recycling saves 95% energy vs primary production. EU aluminium industry invested €700M in recycling facilities. 90%+ recycled content facades demonstrated. Post-consumer recycled content increasing. Reuse of unitised curtain wall units provides additional GWP benefits over recycling. For DPP: recycled content percentage and post-consumer/pre-consumer ratio will be critical environmental data declarations.
structuralIssues.source: Mordor Intelligence (https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-facade-market); European Aluminium (https://european-aluminium.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/sustainability-of-aluminium-in-buildings.pdf); GlassOnWeb circularity (https://www.glassonweb.com/article/circularity-existing-aluminium-unitised-curtain-wall-facades)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and aluminium circular economy in content.

structuralIssues.title: Decopaint Directive + French A+ + German AgBB + EU Ecolabel create fragmented VOC regulatory landscape for CPR harmonisation
structuralIssues.description: Decopaint Directive 2004/42/EC limits VOC content in decorative paints. French 'Émissions dans l'air intérieur' label (mandatory since 2012) rates VOC emissions A+ to C. German AgBB scheme evaluates VOC emissions for indoor suitability (DIN EN 16516/ISO 16000). Belgium, Italy have additional national schemes. EU Ecolabel for paints (Decision 2022/1229) extended to Dec 2025. CPR SReq must harmonise these fragmented VOC approaches into single essential characteristic framework. CEN/TC 139 has extensive EN ISO test methods but no CPR hEN — product standards must be created from scratch referencing existing test methods.
structuralIssues.source: EUR-Lex Decopaint (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=celex:32004L0042); French VOC label (https://www.anses.fr/en/content/labelling-building-and-decoration-products-respect-voc-emissions); AgBB (https://www.eco-institut.de/en/portfolio/agbb-schema/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document fragmented VOC regulatory landscape in content.key_risks. Flag as critical SReq harmonisation challenge.

structuralIssues.title: European paints & coatings $38.7B (2024), decorative 45% — 80% waterborne transition, CEPE sole EU trade body
structuralIssues.description: European paints & coatings market $38.66B (2024). Decorative/architectural 45% of value. Residential construction 48% of end-use. ~80% of European decorative paints now waterborne (driven by Decopaint Directive). CEPE (est. 1951) is sole EU-level trade association. Bio-based coatings and additives emerging. Wall paint is largest construction chemical segment — currently linear waste process. Key players: AkzoNobel, PPG, Sherwin-Williams, Jotun, Hempel, DAW. EU renovation wave (EPBD) drives growth.
structuralIssues.source: Towards Chem & Materials (https://www.towardschemandmaterials.com/insights/european-paints-and-coatings-market); CEPE (https://cepe.org/); European Coatings (https://www.european-coatings.com/news/markets-companies/water-borne-coatings-a-steady-stream-of-developments/)

structuralIssues.title: DWD 2020/2184 parallel framework
structuralIssues.description: EU Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184 is developing a parallel regulatory framework for materials in contact with drinking water. Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/371 (marking) and Implementing Decision (EU) 2024/367 (positive lists) already adopted under DWD. CPR vs DWD jurisdiction for construction products remains unclear.
structuralIssues.action: Document DWD cross-regulatory situation in content sections. Monitor Art. 11 DWD coherence measures.

structuralIssues.title: DWD framework far more advanced than CPR for DWP — six regulations adopted, positive lists apply Dec 2026
structuralIssues.description: EU Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184 implementation is far more advanced than any CPR pathway for DWP. Six delegated/implementing regulations adopted April 2024: European positive lists (Implementing Decision 2024/367, 4 material annexes), testing methodologies (2024/365), marking specifications (Delegated Regulation 2024/371). Positive lists apply 31 Dec 2026 for new products, transitional until 31 Dec 2032. ECHA manages substance lists. CPR Art. 11 coherence requirement means CPR must reference DWD measures. For DPP: any future DWP CPR standards would create cross-regulatory DPP drawing from both EU frameworks simultaneously.
structuralIssues.source: EC press release (https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_24_350); ECHA positive lists (https://echa.europa.eu/european-positive-lists); figawa (https://figawa.org/en/topics/water-supply/harmonisation-of-materials-and-products-in-contact-with-drinking-water/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document DWD regulatory advancement vs CPR dormancy in content. Flag cross-regulatory DPP complexity.

structuralIssues.title: Drinking water pipe/fitting EPDs well-developed but serve PTA family, not DWP
structuralIssues.description: Multiple manufacturers publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs for drinking water products: SANHA (all pipe systems), Uponor (PE100), Pipelife (via EPD Norway), REHAU (PEX), Aquatherm (PP-R green pipe). TEPPFA publishes sector EPDs for PVC-U. EPD coverage for drinking water piping is among the most developed of any construction product category. However, these EPDs primarily serve the PTA family under CPR, not DWP specifically. Material safety data exists under DWD positive lists (ECHA-managed), not CPR framework.
structuralIssues.source: SANHA (https://www.sanha.com/en/blog/sanha-publishes-epds-for-all-pipe-systems-on-its-website-transparency-in-sustainability); REHAU (https://www.rehau.com/downloads/1002828/environmental-product-declaration.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness overlap with PTA in content. Note DWD as primary material safety data source.

structuralIssues.title: European plastics pipe/fittings market $27.3B (2024) — PFAS monitoring from Jan 2026, lead pipe replacement required in Germany
structuralIssues.description: European plastics pipes and pipe fittings market $27.3B (2024). Pipe fittings projected $6.9B by 2030. Drinking water is a key application segment. EU PFAS monitoring in drinking water from 12 Jan 2026 — first systematic EU-wide monitoring. Germany requires lead pipe replacement by 12 Jan 2026 and set first national PFAS drinking water limits. 4MS Initiative (France, Germany, Netherlands, UK) drove EU harmonisation — now ECHA-managed positive lists. Significant product overlap with PTA family.
structuralIssues.source: IndexBox Europe (https://www.indexbox.io/blog/plastics-pipe-and-pipe-fitting-europe-market-overview-2024-4/); EC PFAS rules (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/news/new-eu-rules-limit-pfas-drinking-water-2026-01-12_en)

structuralIssues.title: AVS 4+ joint proposal (Feb 2026) — could change verification regime for DWS
structuralIssues.description: EuroWindoor, European Aluminium, EPPA, Glass for Europe, and SBS jointly proposed a new Assessment and Verification System 'AVS 4+' under CPR 2024/3110 (published 3 Feb 2026). Under AVS 4+, a Notified Body verifies input data and validates software tools, while manufacturers assess performance using the verified digital process within FPC. Could be introduced via delegated act under Art. 10(4). This fundamentally changes the AVCP landscape for DWS and is not currently tracked.
structuralIssues.source: EuroWindoor publications, SBS press release (Feb 2026)

structuralIssues.title: EN 14351 series restructuring into 4 consolidated standards
structuralIssues.description: Under the new SReq, the current EN 14351 series is being reorganised: (1) Pedestrian doorsets = EN 14351-1 (excl. windows) + EN 14351-2 + EN 16034; (2) Windows = EN 14351-1 (excl. doors) + EN 16034; (3) Fire industrial doors = EN 13241 + EN 16034; (4) Non-fire industrial doors = EN 13241. This restructuring means the current 15 hEN listing will change significantly. EN 14351-2 (internal doorsets) has never been OJ-cited since 2018 — a confirmed standardisation deficit.
structuralIssues.source: Efectis article on EN 14351-2, ift Rosenheim new CPR rules, EuroWindoor SReq comments
structuralIssues.action_required: Document restructuring plan in content.standards_development. When new prEN registrations appear at CEN, update standards[] accordingly.

structuralIssues.title: EPBD 2024/1275 creates dual GWP data demand for windows
structuralIssues.description: EPBD recast requires building-level GWP calculations: large new buildings >2000m² from 2027, all new buildings from 2030. Window manufacturers face dual data demand: CPR product-level GWP in DoPC + EPBD building-level contribution via EN 15978/Level(s). Glass industry EPD infrastructure is mature (AGC, Guardian publish EPDs), but window assembly-level data is the bottleneck. Creates urgency for GWP data preparation before new CPR harmonised standards are published.
structuralIssues.source: Directive (EU) 2024/1275 Art. 7(2)-(3) + Annex III (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32024L1275); AGC Glass Europe EPDs (https://www.agc-glass.eu/en/sustainability/environmental-product-declarations); Guardian Glass EPDs (https://www.guardianglass.com/eu/en/our-glass/environmental-product-declarations)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document in content.key_risks. Note that glass industry EPD readiness is ahead of window assembly-level readiness.

structuralIssues.title: PVC/aluminium recycling infrastructure provides DPP end-of-life data foundation

DPP under new CPR requires end-of-life/recyclability data — existing recycling infrastructure provides data foundation. Wood windows have biogenic carbon accounting considerations (EN 15804+A2).
structuralIssues.source: VinylPlus Progress Report 2025 (https://www.vinylplus.eu/progress-report/); EPPA circular economy programme (https://eppa-profiles.eu/circular-economy/); European Aluminium Circular Action Plan (https://european-aluminium.eu/)

structuralIssues.title: CRA reporting obligations from Sep 2026, full compliance Dec 2027
structuralIssues.description: Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) entered into force 10 Dec 2024. Reporting obligations apply from 11 Sep 2026; full compliance from 11 Dec 2027. Euralarm fact sheet (Nov 2024) classifies most electronic fire safety products in 'default' category (self-assessment permitted). Products covered include fire detection control panels, intrusion detection, access control, cloud-based alarm software. Less burdensome than initially assessed but adds cybersecurity essential requirements to product design.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document CRA timeline in content.key_risks. Note self-assessment route for most fire products.

structuralIssues.title: PFAS ban (EU 2025/1988) directly impacts firefighting foam products
structuralIssues.description: Regulation (EU) 2025/1988 (adopted 2 Oct 2025) restricts PFAS in firefighting foams. Portable extinguishers: PFAS ban from 23 Oct 2026. Alcohol-resistant foams: 23 Apr 2027. Full market ban: 23 Oct 2030. Existing equipment: use until 31 Dec 2030. Impacts EN 12094 (gas extinguishing) and EN 12259 (sprinkler/water spray) product families — foam agent compatibility must be reassessed. Creates triple regulatory burden: CPR (product performance) + REACH (chemical restriction) + future DPP (chemical content declaration).
structuralIssues.source: DNV (https://www.dnv.com/news/2025/pfos-prohibited-in-fire-extinguishing-media-from-1-january-2026/); UL Solutions (https://www.ul.com/news/new-eu-restriction-pfas-firefighting-foams); CIRS Group (https://www.cirs-group.com/en/chemicals/eu-adopts-ban-on-pfas-in-firefighting-foam-reach-regulation-amendment-to-take-full-effect-by-2030)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document PFAS ban timeline in content.key_risks. Flag EN 12094 and EN 12259 product compatibility review needed. Monitor fluorine-free foam standard development.

structuralIssues.title: Zero fire product EPDs found — industry must build EPD capability from scratch
structuralIssues.description: No Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) under EN 15804 found for fire detection, fire alarm, sprinkler, or gas extinguishing products. Unlike cement (thousands of EPDs), concrete (hundreds), or steel (dozens), fire safety products have zero EPD infrastructure. DPP GWP declarations under new CPR will require this data. Fire products are complex manufactured goods (electronics, metals, plastics, chemicals) making GWP assessment harder than for single-material products.
structuralIssues.source: EPD International (https://www.environdec.com/services/what-is-epd); BRE Group (https://bregroup.com/services/standards/environmental/en-15804-environmental-product-declaration-epd)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness gap in content. Flag as major DPP implementation risk for fire products.

structuralIssues.title: IoT/networked fire systems blur product/system boundary for DPP
structuralIssues.description: Wireless fire detection market growing from $1.62B (2025) to $3.1B (2035). IoT-integrated fire systems combine alarms + HVAC + access control + BMS. EN 54 was designed for discrete product certification, but networked fire products operate as systems-of-components. DPP faces: per-component EN 54 certification vs system-level IoT integration, triple regulation (CRA cybersecurity + CPR construction performance + ESPR electronics repairability). Mesh-networked sensors and 5G-compatible systems emerging.
structuralIssues.source: FMI market report (https://www.fmiblog.com/2025/06/19/wireless-fire-detection-systems-market-to-reach-usd-3-1-billion-by-2035-driven-by-smart-building-adoption-and-iot-integration/); ScienceDirect IoT fire review (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2542660523001269)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document IoT complexity in content. Consider implications for DPP data model (product vs system granularity).

structuralIssues.title: EAD transition bottleneck quantified: 400 EADs vs 25/yr publishing rate in 3-year window
structuralIssues.description: Trade press (Fastener + Fixing Magazine) quantifies the systemic EAD transition problem: ~400 EADs under current CPR, EOTA publishes 20-30/year, effective transition window is ~3 years (delegated acts ~2027, EADs invalid 2030). Math: 400÷25=16 years vs 3-year window. Industry warns of 'innovation freeze' lasting several years and potential return to national Member State assessments (market fragmentation). Affects all EAD-heavy families. EOTA's 12-month fast-track helps but cannot close the gap. CFE advocates rolling EAD updates instead of deadline-based transition.
structuralIssues.action: Document as systemic risk. Flag for all EAD-heavy families. Consider adding transition risk indicator to tracker.

structuralIssues.title: CBAM + machine-readable EADs + c-PCRs for fixings
structuralIssues.description: Three new cross-regulatory developments: 1) CBAM applies to imported steel fasteners from Jan 2026 (embedded emissions reporting). 2) New CPR requires EADs in machine-readable format for BIM/DPP integration — new digital infrastructure investment needed. 3) Industry exploring complementary Product Category Rules (c-PCRs) to link fixings into LCA/EPD frameworks. Additionally, 5,500+ ETAs exist for construction fixings (largest ETA market), and EN 1992-4 (Eurocode 2 Part 4) creates direct design-code dependency on ETA performance data.
structuralIssues.action: Document in content. Cross-reference with SMP (iss-SMP-006) and RPS (iss-RPS-003) for CBAM consistency.

structuralIssues.title: Hilti leads fixing EPDs (~300 declarations) — Fischer and Würth have no EPDs found
structuralIssues.description: Hilti offers ~300 product declarations including fixing-specific EPDs: HDA undercut anchor (stainless steel, EN 15804+A2), HIT-RE 500 V3/V4 adhesive anchor (ÖKOBAUDAT registered), MT support systems (>80% weight coverage). EcoVadis gold, CO₂ neutral operations since 2023. However, Fischer and Würth — the 2nd and 3rd largest European fixing manufacturers — have no visible EPD programmes for construction fixings. Industry EPD readiness concentrated in market leader only.
structuralIssues.source: Hilti Green Building (https://www.hilti.group/content/hilti/CP/XX/en/company/health-safety-and-environment/products-and-technology/green-building.html); Hilti HIT-RE 500 V4 ÖKOBAUDAT (https://www.oekobaudat.de/OEKOBAU.DAT/resource/sources/8d63ef24-a533-4762-9ec1-24e16020934f/HIT-RE_500_V4_18984.pdf)

structuralIssues.title: Seismic performance categories C1/C2 are safety-critical DPP data fields
structuralIssues.description: European seismic qualification uses ETAG 001 Annex E categories: C1 (non-structural, strength) and C2 (structural, strength + deformation). Seismic data is part of ETA declarations, referenced by EN 1992-4 (Eurocode 2 Part 4). DPP must carry machine-readable seismic qualification to enable designer filtering. Using C1 where C2 is required = structural failure risk in earthquake zones. This is a safety-critical DPP data architecture requirement unique to fixings.
structuralIssues.source: ResearchGate C1/C2 paper (https://www.researchgate.net/publication/346828271_European_Seismic_Performance_Categories_C1_and_C2_for_Post-Installed_Anchors); EOTA ETAG 001 Annex E (https://www.eota.eu/sites/default/files/uploads/ETAGs/etag-001-annex-e-2013-04-08-2.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document seismic DPP data requirements in content. Flag as safety-critical DPP architecture concern.

structuralIssues.title: Vinyl flooring: REACH + CPR dangerous substances + VOC emissions — compound regulatory burden
structuralIssues.description: Vinyl (PVC) flooring faces compound regulation: REACH restricts SVHC phthalates (DEHP), industry shifted to DINCH/DEHA alternatives. New CPR 2024/3110 dangerous substances requirements (CEN/TC 351) will require VOC emission declarations. German AgBB/DIBt method is current benchmark (3/7/14-day VOC protocols). DPP for vinyl must include: VOC emission class, phthalate content, indoor air quality data — alongside traditional performance. This cross-regulatory burden is unique to vinyl within FLO.
structuralIssues.source: EU flooring standards (https://globalfloorings.com/2025/08/11/eu-flooring-standards-and-ce-marking-for-environmental-compliance/); PMC DEHP-free PVC (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6856815/)

structuralIssues.title: EAD expiry (2031) vs SReq timeline (2029) creates regulatory gap risk
structuralIssues.description: EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 but SReq not planned until 2029. Only ~2 years between SReq adoption and EAD expiry — typically the full SReq→development→citation cycle takes 3-4 years. This means new hENs will NOT be available before EADs expire. While ETAs remain valid until 2036 (Art. 95(4) backstop), no new ETAs can be issued after 2031. This creates a gap where new products cannot enter the market via the EAD route and new hENs are not yet available.
structuralIssues.action_required: Flag for policy monitoring. Consider whether Pipeline A NT-9 estimated date (~2030-2032) is realistic given the SReq is planned for 2029.

Dominated by Saint-Gobain (Gyproc, British Gypsum, Rigips), Knauf, and Etex (Siniat, Fermacell). Growth drivers: EPBD renovation wave, fire safety (A1/A2 classification), acoustic performance, modular construction. Emerging alternatives: hempcrete (Hemspan), bio-based panels (Adaptavate). Single-TC structure (CEN/TC 241) and A2-s1,d0 CWFT classification simplify DPP implementation compared to multi-TC families.
structuralIssues.source: GlobeNewsWire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/11/01/2973239/28124/en/Europe-Gypsum-Based-Plasterboard-and-Alternatives-Market-Report-2023-2033); Mordor Intelligence (https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-gypsum-board-market)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context in content for DPP impact assessment.

structuralIssues.title: Only ~15 ETICS EPDs found vs 798 ETAs issued — 50:1 gap in environmental data readiness
structuralIssues.description: Systematic review found only ~15 EPDs for ETICS (EPS, XPS, mineral wool) against 798 issued ETAs. Kit system LCA complexity (insulation + adhesive + mesh + render + fixings) makes EPD boundaries complex. AVCP System 1 ensures structural/fire safety testing but environmental data infrastructure severely lacking. AVCP for ETICS sustainability 'will be challenging to implement in practice based only on environmental indicators according to EN 15804.' Some individual ETICS EPDs exist (Berger S-P-01418 on EPD International).
structuralIssues.source: MDPI Sustainability 13(24):13705 (https://www.mdpi.com/2071-1050/13/24/13705); EPD International (https://www.environdec.com/library/epd1418)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document ETICS EPD readiness gap in content. Flag as major DPP implementation barrier for kit systems.

structuralIssues.title: ETICS market €7.7B + prefab market €78B — combined KAS among largest CPR families by value
structuralIssues.description: ETICS system materials market: $7.73B (2023) → $12.24B by 2031 (CAGR 5.9%). EPS 77% of ETICS insulation. Building renovation 52% of ETICS applications. European prefab buildings market: $78B (2025) → $109.3B by 2030 (CAGR 7%). Timber 36.12% market share. Modular systems 46% revenue share. Germany leads (19.2%). Combined KAS market value rivals largest CPR families. EU Renovation Wave and EPBD targets are primary demand drivers.
structuralIssues.source: Insight Partners ETICS Market (https://www.theinsightpartners.com/reports/europe-etics-system-materials-market); Mordor Intelligence Prefab (https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/european-prefabricated-buildings-industry-study); Fortune BI Modular (https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/europe-modular-construction-market-107747)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context in content for DPP impact assessment.

structuralIssues.title: Kit/system DPP architecture — most complex compound data challenge of any CPR family
structuralIssues.description: KAS kits require 3-layer DPP data: (1) component-level (insulation, adhesive, mesh, render, fixing), (2) system-level (ETICS or prefab unit performance), (3) building-level (EPBD energy calculations). EC CPR DPP feasibility study (May 2025) has limited kit-specific guidance. DPP registry (July 2026) must handle kit-to-component relationships. New EADs require: GWP immediately, LCA-EPD core by 2029, full lifecycle by 2031. Fire safety classification (EN 13501-1, AVCP System 1) is safety-critical system-level DPP data that depends on component interaction.
structuralIssues.source: EC DPP feasibility study (https://op.europa.eu/en/publication-detail/-/publication/cf329d5e-3464-11f0-8a44-01aa75ed71a1/language-en); EC CPR announcement (https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/news/new-eu-rules-safety-and-sustainability-construction-products-mark-new-step-sectors-competitiveness-2025-01-07_en)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document kit DPP architecture challenge in content.key_risks. Flag for DPP system design considerations.

structuralIssues.title: Zero EPDs found for roof ladders, safety hooks, or walkways — metal sector EPD inheritance possible
structuralIssues.description: No EPDs found for fixed roof ladders (EN 12951), roof safety hooks (EN 517), or roof walkways (EN 516). Products are predominantly aluminium and galvanised steel. Could potentially inherit environmental data from aluminium/steel sector EPDs. BAU-EPD lists CEN/TC 128 PCRs suggesting EPD methodology exists at TC level. Niche market size means DPP infrastructure costs may be disproportionately high for manufacturers like ZARGES, Kee Safety, MSA Safety.
structuralIssues.source: European Aluminium (https://european-aluminium.eu/); ZARGES (https://www.zarges.com/uk/fixed-vertical-ladders/); BAU-EPD (https://www.bau-epd.at/en/pcr/list/cen-tc-standards-european-specific-product-category-rules-for-construction-products)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document zero EPD coverage in content. Note metal sector EPD inheritance as potential pathway.

structuralIssues.title: Three-regulation overlap: CPR (EN 12951/516/517), Machinery (EN 14122-4), PPE (EN 353-1) — boundary unclear
structuralIssues.description: Fixed attached ladders sit at the intersection of three EU regulations: CPR 2024/3110 (construction products permanently attached to buildings, EN 12951/EN 516/EN 517), Machinery Regulation (EN 14122-4 fixed ladders on machinery), and PPE Regulation (EN 353-1 guided fall arresters). ZARGES markets fixed ladders to EN 14122-4 rather than EN 12951, suggesting market preference for machinery standard. A fixed ladder attached to a building could need CPR DPP AND machinery/PPE compliance simultaneously. Regulatory boundary needs clarification in SReq (handled within KAS).
structuralIssues.source: ZARGES EN 14122-4 (https://www.zarges.com/uk/fixed-vertical-ladders/); EUR-Lex Directive 2001/45/EC (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32001L0045)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document multi-regulatory overlap in content.key_risks. Flag regulatory boundary as SReq clarification item.

structuralIssues.title: Niche market — ZARGES leads Europe, no market sizing found, EU positions ladders as 'last resort' for height work
structuralIssues.description: No specific market sizing found for fixed attached ladders / roof safety products in Europe — niche segment within broader fall protection market. ZARGES (Germany, 1930s) is Europe's leading manufacturer. Key competitors: Kee Safety, MSA Safety, Layher, Hailo. Materials: aluminium, galvanised steel, stainless steel. EU Directive 2001/45/EC establishes ladders as 'last resort' for work at height — regulatory pressure toward more sophisticated access systems may limit market growth. EN 12951:2004, EN 516:2006, EN 517:2006 are 18-22 years old.
structuralIssues.source: ZARGES (https://www.zarges.com/uk/fixed-vertical-ladders/); EUR-Lex Directive 2001/45/EC (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32001L0045)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and regulatory landscape in content.

structuralIssues.title: EN 998-1 AVCP mismatch with CMG
structuralIssues.description: EN 998-1 in MAS records AVCP as '4' while CMG records '2+, 4'. Per Commission Decision 97/555/EC, the correct AVCP for mortar for masonry is System 2+ for designed mortars and System 4 for prescribed mortars. MAS should likely record '2+, 4' to match. Name format also differs: MAS says 'Mortar for masonry' while CMG says 'Specification for mortar for masonry'.
structuralIssues.action_required: Update MAS EN 998-1 AVCP from '4' to '2+, 4' after verification. Align name format across families.

structuralIssues.title: EN 998-2 DPP estimate differs between MAS and CMG
structuralIssues.description: EN 998-2 in MAS has dpp_est '~2031–2032' while in CMG it is '~2032–2033'. AVCP matches (both '2+, 4'). The difference in DPP estimate is expected since each family derives it from its own SReq timeline, but should be documented as a known variance.
structuralIssues.action_required: Verify DPP estimate derivation for cross-listed standards. Consider adding notes explaining the variance.

structuralIssues.title: Wienerberger leads with EN 15804 EPDs, TBE requires cradle-to-grave — clay strongest, others lag
structuralIssues.description: Wienerberger publishes EN 15804 EPDs for clay masonry products. TBE (Tiles & Bricks Europe) agreement requires all clay product EPDs to be cradle-to-grave (modules A1-D). BAU-EPD publishes PCR Part B for construction clay products. UK BDA publishes industry average clay brick EPD. EPD coverage strongest for clay bricks, less visible for concrete blocks, AAC, and calcium silicate. For DPP: TBE cradle-to-grave requirement means clay EPDs already provide full lifecycle data. Other material types will need to catch up.
structuralIssues.source: Wienerberger EPDs (https://www.wienerberger.co.uk/); TBE sustainability (http://www.tiles-bricks.eu/sustainability); BAU-EPD PCR (https://www.bau-epd.at/)

structuralIssues.title: Clay brick reuse increasing, K-Briq 90% recycled, EAD 170005 enables recycled clay CE route
structuralIssues.description: Clay brick reuse from demolition increasing. Crushed brick as aggregate replacement. K-Briq: 90% C&D waste, 5% embodied carbon of traditional clay brick. EU 70% CDW recycling target. EAD 170005 (recycled clay masonry units) provides CE-marking route for recycled content. EAD 170051 (compressed earth blocks) enables low-energy earth construction. EAD 170012 (cellular glass core bricks) offers thermal innovation. MAS EADs represent some of the most circular-economy-oriented products in CPR.
structuralIssues.source: BDA circular principles (https://www.brick.org.uk/better-with-brick/sustainability/circular-principles); UKGBC (https://ukgbc.org/resources/bricks-made-from-industrial-waste/); EOTA EAD 170005 (https://www.eota.eu/)

structuralIssues.title: European AAC $1.75B, clay 41% — Wienerberger electric kiln achieves nearly CO₂-neutral brick production
structuralIssues.description: European AAC market $1.75B (2024), CAGR 5.5%. Clay bricks 41% of European market. Wienerberger inaugurated world's largest electric kiln at Uttendorf, Austria (Nov 2024) — nearly CO₂-neutral brick production with 90% emission reduction. Running on green electricity. First nearly carbon-neutral clay brick. Wienerberger targets: 25% production emission reduction by 2026, climate neutrality by 2050. structuralIssues.title: Market leaders publish EN 15804 EPDs, prEN 17388-2 membrane-specific PCR under development
structuralIssues.description: Sika (SikaProof, Sikaplan), BMI Group (Icopal), Bauder (RBM systems), and Soprema all publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs for waterproofing membranes. prEN 17388-2 'Flexible sheets for waterproofing — Environmental product declarations — Product Category Rules' is under development at CEN/TC 254 — this membrane-specific PCR would harmonise EPD methodology across bitumen and synthetic sheets, directly supporting DPP environmental data generation. SME manufacturers likely lack EPD infrastructure. For DPP: EPD readiness among market leaders is relatively strong for a late-timeline family, but the PCR is needed to ensure comparability across material types.

structuralIssues.title: PVC recycling via ROOFCOLLECT, EPDM recycled, but bitumen membranes mostly landfilled
structuralIssues.description: ROOFCOLLECT (ESWA programme) coordinates recovery and recycling of post-consumer PVC roofing membranes across Europe. VinylPlus recycled ~1M tonnes PVC annually. EPDM recycled into rubber crumb for tires, pavers, playgrounds. Recycled content in new PVC membranes limited to ~5%. Bitumen membranes are the most challenging to recycle — most end up in landfill or energy recovery. For DPP: circular economy data will vary dramatically by membrane material within this single family — PVC has established collection infrastructure, bitumen has almost none.
structuralIssues.source: ROOFCOLLECT/ESWA; VinylPlus annual reports
structuralIssues.action_required: Document material-dependent recycling gap in content. Flag bitumen end-of-life as DPP circular economy challenge.

structuralIssues.title: European waterproofing membrane market $4.6B (2024) — bitumen dominant but PVC/TPO gaining, material split creates DPP complexity
structuralIssues.description: European waterproofing membrane market $4.6B (2024), growing at 4.1–5.9% CAGR. Global market projected beyond $14.5B by 2032. Bitumen sheets remain dominant in continental Europe but PVC and TPO single-ply membranes gaining share (lighter weight, faster installation, cool roof properties). EPDM holds niche position. Key manufacturers: Sika, BMI Group, Soprema, IKO, Bauder. Below-grade waterproofing growing segment driven by EU infrastructure investment ($95.5B+ EIB urban regeneration). National regulatory fragmentation (e.g., DIN 18533 vs EN 13967) adds complexity for DPP declared performance.
structuralIssues.source: GMInsights (https://www.gminsights.com/pressrelease/waterproofing-membrane-market); ScienceDirect (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950061821015117)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and material split DPP implications in content.

structuralIssues.title: GWP declaration mandatory from Jan 2026 for EN 1520 and EN 12602
structuralIssues.description: Under new CPR 2024/3110, GWP declarations became mandatory from 8 January 2026 for products covered by existing OJ-cited harmonised standards. EN 1520:2011 and EN 12602:2016 are both OJ-cited under M/100, making precast concrete manufacturers among the first affected. Creates early compliance burden before full DPP system operational. Strong EPD infrastructure (EN 15804) eases transition.

structuralIssues.title: GCCA Low Carbon Ratings for concrete (incl. precast) launched Apr 2025
structuralIssues.description: GCCA Global Ratings for Concrete: 7-band AA-G classification based on GWP (kg CO₂e/m³) cross-referenced with strength (MPa). Precast explicitly included in scope. Band E = global reference (e.g., 255 kg CO₂e/m³ for 20 MPa). Uses EN 15804+A2. UK adaptation published Feb 2025. Provides voluntary carbon class overlay alongside mandatory CPR GWP disclosure in DoPC.
structuralIssues.source: GCCA Concrete Ratings (https://gccassociation.org/lcr-concrete/); GCCA Definitions PDF (https://gccassociation.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/GCCA_Concrete_Definitions_for_Low_Carbon_and_NearZeroPolicy_Digital.pdf)

structuralIssues.description: TEPPFA has developed an EPD application tool for plastic piping. structuralIssues.title: Triple microplastics regulation creates cross-regulatory pressure on plastic pipe manufacturing
structuralIssues.description: Three EU regulations affect plastic pipe manufacturing: (1) REACH 2023/2055 restricts intentionally added microplastics (phased from Oct 2025). (2) EU 2025/2365 (16 Dec 2025) targets plastic pellet losses at manufacturing facilities handling 5+ tonnes/year. (3) Drinking Water Directive recast requires new leaching/microbial standards for pipe materials by 31 Dec 2026. While PTA covers pipes NOT in contact with drinking water, the regulatory trend tightens around all plastic products. DPP may eventually need microplastics/leaching data fields.
structuralIssues.source: EC microplastics (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/plastics/microplastics_en); REACH 2023/2055 (https://en.reach24h.com/news/insights/chemical/eu-microplastics-spm-restriction-deadline); EU 2025/2365 (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/news/new-law-reducing-microplastic-pollution-enters-force-2025-12-16_en); DWD recast (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/water/drinking-water_en)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document cross-regulatory microplastics pressure in content.key_risks. Note pellet regulation impact on pipe manufacturers.

structuralIssues.title: Eurobitume LCA 4.0 (2025) doubles bitumen GWP to 530 kg CO₂e/t — all DPP data must use updated figure
structuralIssues.description: Eurobitume LCA Report 4.0 (March 2025) updates paving-grade bitumen GWP100 from 216 kg CO₂e/t (LCA 3.1, 2021) to 530 kg CO₂e/t — a 145% increase. Change is due to improved methane accounting from crude oil extraction, not increased actual emissions. Data from 17 European refineries (75%+ coverage). Outputs are EN 15804+A2 compatible. ~70% of bitumen GWP stems from oil extraction and refining. All future RCP EPDs and DPP environmental declarations must use this updated figure. Previous EPDs using LCA 3.1 data are significantly understated.
structuralIssues.source: Eurobitume LCA 4.0 (https://eurobitume.eu/lca_4-0_news/); Eurobitume LCA 4.0 PDF (https://eurobitume.eu/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/EB-LCA-4.0-2025.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Flag Eurobitume LCA 4.0 GWP revision in content. Note EN 15804+A2 compatibility for DPP readiness.

structuralIssues.title: RAP recycling infrastructure provides DPP recycled content data — 45.5 Mt/yr available, 76% reused
structuralIssues.description: Reclaimed Asphalt Pavement (RAP) is Europe's most recycled construction material. 45.5 Mt available annually, 23.2 Mt utilised in asphalt production, 76% reused in mixes. 65-75% goes to new asphalt, 25-35% to unbound layers, ~5% to landfill. 100% recyclability demonstrated. RAP incorporation typically limited to 10-30% by national specifications. EN 13108-8 governs RAP specifications. This circular economy data is directly relevant to DPP recycled content and end-of-life data fields.
structuralIssues.source: EAPA Recycling (https://eapa.org/recycling/); EAPA Asphalt in Figures (https://eapa.org/asphalt-in-figures/); CAPRI RAP overview (https://www.capri-project.com/news-1/the-importance-of-re-userecycle-rap-quick-overview-in-europe)

EPBD obligation will massively increase BIPV demand and regulatory scrutiny. Transposition deadline 29 May 2026.

structuralIssues.title: BIPV roof products need dual DPP data: CPR construction + IEC electrical performance

structuralIssues.title: PTFE is a PFAS — proposed EU universal restriction could affect structural bearing production
structuralIssues.description: PTFE (polytetrafluoroethylene) is the primary sliding material in structural bearings and is classified as a PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substance). The proposed EU universal PFAS restriction could affect PTFE bearing production if critical-use exemptions are not granted. UHMWPE and modified polyamide alternatives exist but may not match PTFE performance at high temperatures (UHMWPE degrades >80°C). 7 of 8 SBE EADs cover innovative bearing materials including UHMWPE and fluoropolymer alternatives — these EADs may become strategically important if PTFE is restricted. Bearing material substitution would require re-testing and new CE marking.
structuralIssues.source: PTFE PFAS debate (https://polyfluoroltd.com/blog/ptfe-and-the-pfas-debate-why-one-polymer-deserves-a-closer-look/); DREYPLAS alternatives (https://www.dreyplas.com/en/better-than-ptfe-and-uhmwpe/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document PFAS restriction risk in content.key_risks. Monitor EU PFAS restriction timeline for PTFE exemption status.

Around 10% of European bridges significantly deficient, 30% not regularly surveyed. Total built value ~€2 trillion, annual maintenance needed ~€30B/year. 35+ bridges adopted sensor-integrated bearings in 2024. CEF budget €33.71B (2021-2027). Ageing infrastructure drives bearing replacement demand. Sensor-integrated bearings and digital twin monitoring emerging — potential future DPP extension to in-service performance data.
structuralIssues.source: Bridge bearings market (https://www.openpr.com/news/4340139/bridge-bearings-market-valued-at-3-28-billion-in-2024); Construction Briefing (https://www.constructionbriefing.com/news/europes-ageing-bridges-are-crumbling-what-can-be-done-to-prevent-collapses/8036646.article)

structuralIssues.title: VOC + REACH + CEN/TC 351 = compound regulatory burden for sealants, especially PU formulations
structuralIssues.description: Construction sealants face compound regulation: VOC emission limits (AgBB/DIBt strictest), REACH SVHC restrictions (isocyanates for PU, phthalates), and CEN/TC 351 dangerous substances horizontal standards under new CPR M/366. DPP declarations will need: VOC emission class, SVHC content, indoor air quality data alongside mechanical performance. CEN/TC 351 will standardise dangerous substance data generation methods for DPP.
structuralIssues.source: CEN/TC 351 (https://www.cencenelec.eu/news-events/events/2023/2023-05-10-cen-tc-351-conference-riga/); REACH (https://echa.europa.eu/substances-restricted-under-reach)

Templates cover product properties, GS1 identifiers (GTIN, GLN, GMN), EPD data, and circularity data. GS1 identifiers align with CPR DPP unique product identifier requirements. Industry DPP groundwork is underway but manufacturer adoption level is unclear.
structuralIssues.source: CEI-Bois Digital Templates (https://www.cei-bois.org/digital-templates); bSDD Wood Dictionary (https://search.bsdd.buildingsmart.org/uri/cei-bois.org/wood/1.0.0); Swedish Wood TIMBIM launch (https://www.swedishwood.com/about_us/news/2021/3/the-european-timber-industry-is-starting-its-digitization-initiative/)
structuralIssues.action: Document in content. Assess whether TIMBIM templates map to CPR DPP data requirements. Monitor manufacturer adoption.

structuralIssues.title: CLT fire safety delamination creates national regulatory divergences affecting DPP data
structuralIssues.description: CLT susceptible to delamination at elevated temperatures — adhesive failure causes cyclical burning, no reliable self-extinguishment (MDPI Fire, CROSS-UK Report 966). EN 1995-1-2:2025 published with CLT-specific charring rules (0.65 mm/min). Arup 'Code Red' research found CLT ceiling fire spread faster than predicted. National regulatory approaches diverge significantly (UK cautious, Austria positive, France mixed). DPP fire performance data may need national context.
structuralIssues.source: MDPI Fire CLT review (https://www.mdpi.com/2571-6255/8/10/406); CROSS-UK Report 966 (https://www.cross-safety.org/uk/safety-information/cross-safety-report/risk-collapse-multi-storey-clt-buildings-during-966); Arup Mass Timber Fire Guide (https://www.arup.com/en-us/insights/fire-safe-design-of-mass-timber-buildings/)
structuralIssues.action: Document fire safety regulatory divergence in content.key_risks. Monitor national building code developments for CLT.

structuralIssues.title: CPR Working Plan 2026-2029 confirms STP NOT in first DPP cohort
structuralIssues.description: CPR Working Plan (16 Dec 2025) sequences DPP rollout: DPP Registry Jul 2026, Service Provider rules Q4 2026, CEM first DPP-ready hENs Q4 2027, DWS 2028-2029. STP is Priority 7 overall but is NOT among the first DPP cohort. STP DPP timeline likely 2029+. CPR DPP Feasibility Study (May 2025) recommends decentralised system with authorised service providers.
structuralIssues.source: Cobuilder CPR Working Plan analysis (https://cobuilder.com/en/digital-product-passport-dpp/digital-product-passports-for-construction-what-the-eus-2026-2029-cpr-working-plan-means-for-stakeholders/); EU Publications CPR DPP Feasibility Study (https://op.europa.eu/en/publication-detail/-/publication/cf329d5e-3464-11f0-8a44-01aa75ed71a1/language-en)
structuralIssues.action: Update DPP timeline context. Document CPR Working Plan sequencing in content.

structuralIssues.title: Knauf, Rockwool, EUMEPS all publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs — strong infrastructure for mineral wool + EPS
structuralIssues.description: Knauf Insulation publishes comprehensive EN 15804+A2 EPDs for glass and rock mineral wool (multiple λ ranges). Rockwool publishes EN 15804+A2 EPDs. EUMEPS publishes industry-wide sector EPDs for EPS insulation (updated 2024, multiple densities, IBU-verified). EUMEPS actively participating in c-PCR EPD revision for thermal insulation. Sector EPD approach enables SME EPS producers to declare DPP environmental data. XPS, PUR/PIR, phenolic foam EPD coverage less visible. Mineral wool (55% of EU market) and EPS (~25%) are well-covered; other materials lag.
structuralIssues.source: Knauf Rock Mineral Wool EPDs (https://www.knaufinsulation.com/downloads/environmental-product-declarations-epd/rock-mineral-wool-rmw); EUMEPS EPD 2024 (https://eumeps.eu/eumeps-newsroom-sie/publications/environmental-product-declaration-for-eps-insulation-2024); EUMEPS c-PCR (https://www.eumeps.eu/eumeps-newsroom-sie/news/collaboration-in-standardisation-navigating-the-c-pcr-epd-revision-for-thermal-insulation)

structuralIssues.title: Majority of insulation waste landfilled — EPS ETICS waste to triple by 2050, France EPR leading
structuralIssues.description: Currently majority of insulation C&D waste is landfilled (up to €400/tonne). EPS waste from ETICS expected to almost double by 2030 and triple by 2050 as post-1970s insulated buildings reach end-of-life. Knauf RESULATION project converts mineral wool waste into secondary raw materials. France Anti-Waste Law introduces EPR for building materials. Austria plans insulation landfill ban from 2027. LIFE ReWo project (EU-funded) recycling mineral wool into high-value products. For DPP: end-of-life recyclability data and recycled content declarations will be critical.
structuralIssues.source: Eurima circular economy (https://www.eurima.org/circular-economy); Knauf RESULATION (https://www.resulation.eu/en); LIFE ReWo (https://webgate.ec.europa.eu/life/publicWebsite/project/LIFE22-ENV-IT-LIFE-ReWo-101113855/recycling-mineral-wool-waste-into-high-value-products)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document insulation waste challenge in content.key_risks. Flag France EPR and Austria landfill ban as DPP-relevant regulatory developments.

structuralIssues.title: European insulation $21B (2024), CAGR 6.32% — EPBD renovation wave targeting 35M buildings by 2030
structuralIssues.description: European insulation market $21.03B (2024), projected to $36.52B by 2033 (CAGR 6.32%). Building thermal insulation specifically $10.11B (2024). EPBD renovation wave targeting ~35 million buildings by 2030, nearly doubling current annual rate. Market fragmentation: mineral wool 55%, EPS ~25%, XPS/PUR/PIR ~20%. TIP among largest CPR families by value. DPP must accommodate fundamentally different material types within one family — similar challenge to FLO.
