structuralIssues.title: REACH E0.5 formaldehyde threshold impacts WBP from August 2026
structuralIssues.description: Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/1464 amends REACH Annex XVII, halving the formaldehyde emission limit for wood-based panels from 0.124 mg/m³ to 0.062 mg/m³ (E0.5 class), effective August 2026. This directly impacts EN 13986 formaldehyde classification and testing requirements. While not a CPR change, it affects future DPP data content (emission declarations) and may accelerate EN 13986 revision needs.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document in content.key_risks. Consider adding a cross_regulatory field or note to the WBP data model.

structuralIssues.title: COM(2025) 772 merged STP and WBP workstreams
structuralIssues.description: The CPR Working Plan COM(2025) 772 merged structural timber products (family 13/STP, priority 7) and wood-based panels (family 14/WBP, priority 16) workstreams. WBP milestones may be derived from STP's timeline rather than independently set. This partially explains the empty milestones in WBP (iss-WBP-002). The merger creates a dependency: WBP progress depends on STP workstream pace. STP cannot use fast-track but WBP can — may need separate tracks during standards development.
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor whether WBP gets independent milestones or follows STP. Document merger in content.sreq_analysis.

structuralIssues.title: SG 20 confirmed 3-year minimum for wood standards; 2040 full transition
structuralIssues.description: 30th SG 20 meeting (7 May 2025) confirmed wood product standards are 'at minimum 3 years away.' Full CPR transition target is 2040 (gradual by product group). Until new harmonised standards published, old CPR applies — dual-system period. Notified body obligations expanded under new CPR.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document SG 20 findings in content. Update DPP outlook with 2040 backstop context.

structuralIssues.title: WBP is priority 16/34 with fast-track available
structuralIssues.description: WBP is priority 16 of 34 product families per COM(2025) 772. Unlike STP (priority 7, no fast-track), WBP has fast-track standards updating available. Fast-track not yet initiated. Could theoretically accelerate standards updating once SReq bottleneck is cleared. Priority 16 explains why milestones are absent in first working plan cycle.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document priority and fast-track in content. Consider impact on DPP range estimate.

structuralIssues.title: Kronospan lacks EPDs for highest-volume products — industry readiness varies dramatically
structuralIssues.description: Kronospan (largest EU panel producer) has NO EPD for particleboard or MFC — its highest-volume products. Meanwhile Pfleiderer already converted entire German production to E0.5, achieved 53.5% post-consumer recycled content, and offers OrganicBoard Pure (100% renewable binder + 100% recycled fibre). EGGER has IBU-verified EPDs and 66% recycled wood. Industry EPD readiness varies dramatically and will be exposed by DPP rollout.
structuralIssues.source: EPD Guide Kronospan analysis (https://epd.guide/manufacturers/kronospan-luxembourg-product-range-and-epd-reality); Pfleiderer materials (https://www.pfleiderer.com/global-en/sustainability/materials); EGGER product transparency (https://www.egger.com/en/about-us/sustainability/product-transparency?country=US)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document readiness disparity in content. Flag Kronospan EPD gap as risk for CPR GWP disclosure.

structuralIssues.title: Triple regulatory overlay: REACH E0.5 + CPR dangerous substances + national IAQ schemes
structuralIssues.description: WBP faces three overlapping formaldehyde/VOC regimes: (1) REACH E0.5 (0.062 mg/m³, 6 Aug 2026) — market access restriction; (2) CPR dangerous substances SReq via CEN/TC 351 (15 deliverables due 31 Dec 2029) — declaration requirement, CPR still references E1 creating mismatch; (3) National IAQ schemes operating in parallel with different thresholds: Germany AgBB 2024, France mandatory A+ labelling, Belgium flooring decree, Italy CAM. EU-LCI harmonised value for formaldehyde has been POSTPONED pending ECHA classification.
structuralIssues.source: REACH (EU) 2023/1464 (https://www.ul.com/news/new-eu-restriction-formaldehyde-regulation-eu-20231464); AgBB 2024 (https://www.eco-institut.de/en/2024/10/federal-environment-agency-publication-agbb-scheme-2024/); EU-LCI values (https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/sectors/construction/eu-lci-subgroup/eu-lci-values_en); ANSES France (https://www.anses.fr/en/content/labelling-building-and-decoration-products-respect-voc-emissions)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document triple overlay in content.key_risks. Note E1/E0.5 CPR-REACH mismatch. Monitor EU-LCI formaldehyde derivation.

structuralIssues.title: Waste wood classification NOT harmonised — DPP recycled content declaration obstacle
structuralIssues.description: No unified EU legislative framework for waste wood classification. Recycled content varies: Italy ~95% in particleboard, Belgium/Denmark 50-70%, Germany/France/Spain 15-30%, EU average ~25%. EPF standard limits contamination to 2% dry weight, but countries apply different categories (Germany AltholzV I-IV, others use A-D). Lack of harmonised classification is a direct obstacle for DPP recycled content declarations. 38M m³ post-consumer wood recovered globally (2022), Europe 82.9%.
structuralIssues.source: PMC Sustainability in WBP (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11499423/); CEPS waste wood circularity (https://cdn.ceps.eu/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/2024-14_ERCC_Wood2Wood.pdf); EU Circular Economy Platform (https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/en/knowledge/improving-waste-wood-circularity-eu-classification-frameworks-and-policy-options)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document waste wood classification gap in content. Flag as DPP implementation obstacle. Monitor EU harmonisation efforts.

structuralIssues.title: New EU facade fire test standard effective 31 January 2026 — post-Grenfell system-level testing
structuralIssues.description: Revised European cladding fire test standard takes effect 31 Jan 2026. Post-Grenfell: minimum Euroclass A2-s1,d0 for buildings >11m. EOTA TR 078 provides system-level fire assessment methodology beyond EN 13823 SBI single-product testing. DPP fire classification data (Euroclass, smoke, droplets) is safety-critical and must be machine-readable. AVCP System 1 for fire classes A1-C ensures third-party testing. May require re-testing existing products.

structuralIssues.title: UWWTD recast extends to 1,000 PE — directly increases EN 12566 product demand and registration aligns with DPP
structuralIssues.description: EU Urban Waste Water Treatment Directive recast (EU) 2024/3019 (in force 1 Jan 2025) extends coverage to agglomerations from 1,000 PE (previously 2,000). Individual treatment systems must be registered and subject to risk-based inspections. Collecting systems required for 1,000-2,000 PE agglomerations by 2035. Transposition deadline 31 Jul 2027. This directly increases the addressable market for EN 12566 products (estimated 20,000+ additional agglomerations). The UWWTD registration requirement aligns with DPP product traceability — treatment efficiency data could become part of DPP in-service performance declarations.
structuralIssues.source: European Commission (https://environment.ec.europa.eu/news/new-rules-urban-wastewater-management-set-enter-force-2024-12-20_en); EUR-Lex (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2024/3019/oj/eng)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document UWWTD recast impact in content.key_risks. Flag cross-regulatory alignment with DPP traceability.

structuralIssues.title: EPD coverage for WWD-scoped products minimal — Geberit has drainage EPDs but not for EN 12566 products
structuralIssues.description: Geberit publishes EN 15804 EPDs for PE/HDPE drainage pipes (34% of Group sales with EPDs). However, EPDs for WWD-scoped products (septic tanks, packaged treatment plants, grease separators, light-liquid separators) were not found. The small wastewater treatment sector lacks the EPD infrastructure seen in building materials families. Manufacturers of EN 12566 products (e.g., Klaro, BioDisc, Clearwater) do not appear to have published EN 15804 EPDs. For DPP: environmental data generation will need to start from scratch for most WWD manufacturers.
structuralIssues.source: Geberit PE EPD (https://files.etim-mapper.com/branchekataloget/4553/epd-pe-group-en.pdf); Geberit sustainability (https://www.geberit-global.com/know-how/sustainability/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD gap in content. Flag as DPP readiness challenge for WWD manufacturers.
